applied.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, ui overlay, crowded scene, many small objects, unreadable background detail, motion blur, action pose, blockbuster cinematic lighting, poster design, collage fragments, multiple surreal events, competing bright accents, icicle, icicles, hanging ice, frozen spikes, stalactite ice, snow drift, blizzard, heavy snow, storm drama
applied.positive_prompt: restrained documentary-surreal image, one clear subject or scene, subject remains recognizable after simplification, one dominant anomaly or contradiction, controlled palette, restrained lighting, clear silhouette, low clutter, non-spectacular tension, Kennedy Space Center launch complex at first light seen from across the water, Challenger stack and fixed service structure rising from the flat coastal pad, the site remains calm and fully recognizable, blue-gray air and thin cryogenic haze soften the lower platform, damp deck surfaces and a faint rim frost sit on rails and edges only, no hanging icicles, no snow, muted sodium work lights, cold documentary stillness

notes: Derived from the official ComfyUI Flux.2 Dev text-to-image workflow.
Adapted to expose negative_prompt, batch_size, seed, and filename_prefix for this lab.
The negative prompt branch is retained as an unconnected CLIPTextEncode node because the official FLUX.2 Dev text-to-image path is positive-guidance-first.
Export-time model loader filenames are patched from FluxLab runtime config so local-safe checkpoints can replace the official quantized default.
